clinical_validity.rationale: The plan uses public Step 2 CS structure, AMA day-one skill targets, specialty physicians, and human clinical review, while avoiding confidential exam content.

psychometric_defensibility.rationale: The plan includes blueprint coverage, case-specific rubrics, rater calibration, trace completeness, and pilot exports, but validation studies are not yet designed.

technical_feasibility.rationale: The TypeScript/Node/MongoDB architecture is implementable and staged, with explicit spikes for XR, ASR, TTS, LLM consistency, and CellixJS fit.

architecture_coherence.rationale: The exam blueprint, form, station, actor card, actor-cell runtime, trace ledger, and review lifecycle are now coherent enough for implementation planning.

security_privacy.rationale: The design adds consent, audit, retention, tenant boundaries, and no real-patient data, but still needs a formal threat model and data-flow appendix.

ux_workflow_fit.rationale: Learner, faculty, scenario author, psychometric reviewer, admin, and debrief workflows are documented with station lifecycle and review ergonomics.

cost_performance_efficiency.rationale: The design separates hot-path dialogue from background review and supports mock providers, but real speech/avatar/LLM cost envelopes remain unmeasured.

open_source_sustainability.rationale: The monorepo and source-ledger posture are sustainable, while CellixJS dependency, license posture, and contribution model remain open.

market_gtm_strength.rationale: The design has a stronger institutional training wedge around sequential clinical skills and faculty review, but external claims still need buyer evidence.

evidence_discipline.rationale: New source records ground public exam structure, AMA skill targets, LLM virtual patient precedent, NBME patient-note caution, and CellixJS forethought.

implementation_readiness.rationale: The docs include data models, statecharts, sequences, UX flows, MADRs, and a phased handoff.

adversarial_robustness.rationale: This is the pre-challenge core plan; it anticipates many constraints but still needs the adversarial team to stress latency, drift, overclaiming, and validation.

legal_regulatory_resilience.rationale: The plan blocks equivalence/licensure claims, requires legal review, and preserves source provenance, but counsel packet artifacts remain to be drafted.

specialty_clinical_generalizability.rationale: Specialty physician agents and station templates cover ED, inpatient, clinic, pediatrics, ICU, telehealth, OB/GYN, psychiatry, surgery, and internal medicine patterns.

critical_risks.summary: Formal validation evidence is absent; score use must remain formative or local until psychometric studies are completed.
Real-time multimodal latency and actor consistency could make multi-actor XR stations unusable without strong fallback modes.
External messaging must not imply USMLE, ECFMG, NBME, AMA, or licensure equivalence or endorsement.

evidence_debt.summary: The supplied IEEE document link could not be fully resolved into a source record and needs manual library access or alternate citation discovery.
Measure current Quest/WebXR, ASR, TTS, avatar, and frontier LLM latency for a realistic multi-actor station.
Draft validation-study plan before any summative or high-stakes score-use claim.

decision_debt.summary: Choose whether the first implementation demo executes one station, three stations, or a full 12-station dry run.
Decide whether CellixJS is a hard dependency, a pattern influence, or a deferred adapter after the technology spike.
Select first real LLM, ASR, and TTS provider adapters after safety, latency, cost, and auditability testing.

summary: The core plan is now development-ready as an architecture packet for a source-backed, Step 2 CS-inspired, multi-station XR clinical skills exam system, with unresolved validation, latency, and legal-claims risks made explicit.
